rulings.Case Title: BETTY PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, JANUARY 4, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Turner Construction Co.¿s motion for summary judgment and motion for summary adjudication are continued to January 11 per stipulation of the parties. Stipulation & proposed order to be presented at the hearing at 10:15am, not 9:30am. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 4, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Rountree Plumbing & Heating Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of production. Plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories and Mr. Cantley¿s deposition testimony taken in this case preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Mr. Cantley¿s deposition testimony taken in this case is admissible and defendant failed to demonstrate that it contradicts any of his prior testimony. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Peterson was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, Mr. Cantley¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Peterson was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Even if Mr. Singewald¿s and Ms. Haldeman¿s declarations are considered, they merely create a triable issue when considered in conjunction with Mr. Cantley¿s deposition testimony and declaration. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: SHARON DUNN et al VS. AMCHEM PRODUCTS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 4, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Merlex Stucco, Inc.¿s motion to strike portions of the complaint relating to punitive damages is off calendar as moot given the ruling on defendant¿s demurrer. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: SHARON DUNN et al VS. AMCHEM PRODUCTS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 4, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Merlex Stucco, Inc.¿s general demurrer to plaintiffs¿ complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. Defendant¿s special demurrer to the sixth cause of action (loss of consortium) is overruled. Plaintiffs¿ complaint sufficiently pleads a cause of action for loss of consortium and the authorities cited by defendant on that topic are inapplicable. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
